evals.name: fintech-budgeting
evals.prompt: ok we're building a budgeting app for young people in mexico, it's called Peso. i want it to feel trustworthy but friendly, not like a stuffy bank. main color a deep teal, maybe a lime green accent, clean modern headings. can you build us a design system we can reuse across our clickable prototypes and our actual Fresh app? we'll be making dashboards mostly.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained 'Peso Design System' folder: canonical theme.css (daisyUI-5 brand+brand-dark, deep-teal primary, lime accent), derived theme.cdn.css/manifest.json/adherence.oxlintrc.json, docs (README/design-tokens/components/charts), consume/prototype.md, preview specimens incl. a showcase dashboard, and its own SKILL.md.
expectations: Produces a canonical theme.css with two @plugin "daisyui/theme" blocks (light 'brand' + dark 'brand-dark') including the full required daisyUI-5 variable set: base-100/200/300, base-content, primary/secondary/accent/neutral/info/success/warning/error each with a -content pair, radius-selector/field/box, size-selector/field, border, depth, noise.
theme.css includes the non-color layer daisyUI does not cover: --font-display and --font-body, a --step-* type scale, --ease-* motion tokens, and color-mix() shade tokens.

Ships derived files theme.cdn.css, manifest.json, and adherence.oxlintrc.json, each marked generated/derived from theme.css; theme.cdn.css uses [data-theme="brand"] blocks whose color values match theme.css.
Ships consume/prototype.md; the prototype recipe specifies BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script.
components.md uses daisyUI-5 classes and contains NONE of the removed-in-v5 classes input-bordered, form-control, or label-text.
preview/showcase.html includes BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script, sets data-theme="brand", and uses daisyUI semantic classes.
Includes charts.md with theme-native ApexCharts that read theme colors via var(--color-*) or getComputedStyle, not hardcoded hex.
Ships a SKILL.md (artifact is itself invokable) and a README brand bible; the daisyUI theme is named brand/brand-dark (generic), not the literal brand name.
The chosen primary reads as a deep teal and the accent as lime per the brief; the body/display fonts are distinctive (not defaulting to Inter/Roboto/system).
showcase.html renders as a coherent themed dashboard (sidebar + KPI stats + chart + table) with the brand primary on the main CTA/KPIs.

evals.name: wellness-meditation
evals.prompt: I run a small meditation + sleep studio called Lumen and I want a cohesive design system for our marketing site and our app. The feeling should be calm, soft, airy — think lavender and warm sand, lots of breathing room, a gentle humanist serif for headings and a clean sans for body. Make it something our designers and our Fresh app can both use.
evals.expected_output: A 'Lumen Design System' folder with a calm lavender/sand daisyUI-5 theme (serif display + sans body), the full derived/doc/consume/preview structure, and a showcase that reads soft and airy.
expectations: Produces a canonical theme.css with two @plugin "daisyui/theme" blocks (light 'brand' + dark 'brand-dark') including the full required daisyUI-5 variable set: base-100/200/300, base-content, primary/secondary/accent/neutral/info/success/warning/error each with a -content pair, radius-selector/field/box, size-selector/field, border, depth, noise.
theme.css includes the non-color layer daisyUI does not cover: --font-display and --font-body, a --step-* type scale, --ease-* motion tokens, and color-mix() shade tokens.

Ships derived files theme.cdn.css, manifest.json, and adherence.oxlintrc.json, each marked generated/derived from theme.css; theme.cdn.css uses [data-theme="brand"] blocks whose color values match theme.css.
Ships consume/prototype.md; the prototype recipe specifies BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script.
components.md uses daisyUI-5 classes and contains NONE of the removed-in-v5 classes input-bordered, form-control, or label-text.
preview/showcase.html includes BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script, sets data-theme="brand", and uses daisyUI semantic classes.
Includes charts.md with theme-native ApexCharts that read theme colors via var(--color-*) or getComputedStyle, not hardcoded hex.
Ships a SKILL.md (artifact is itself invokable) and a README brand bible; the daisyUI theme is named brand/brand-dark (generic), not the literal brand name.
The display font is a humanist serif and the body a clean sans per the brief; the palette reads as lavender + warm sand (calm/airy), not generic.
showcase.html renders as a coherent themed surface that feels soft/airy, with the brand colors applied via semantic classes.

evals.name: gigwork-marketplace
evals.prompt: we're launching Chamba, a gig-work marketplace for tradespeople — electricians, plumbers, etc. it needs to feel bold, energetic, get-it-done. bright orange, strong near-black, chunky type. build a reusable design system / theme for it — we use the prototype skill a lot and also have a deno fresh app.
evals.expected_output: A 'Chamba Design System' folder with a bold orange/near-black daisyUI-5 theme (chunky display type), the full derived/doc/consume/preview structure, and an energetic showcase.
expectations: Produces a canonical theme.css with two @plugin "daisyui/theme" blocks (light 'brand' + dark 'brand-dark') including the full required daisyUI-5 variable set: base-100/200/300, base-content, primary/secondary/accent/neutral/info/success/warning/error each with a -content pair, radius-selector/field/box, size-selector/field, border, depth, noise.

Ships consume/prototype.md; the prototype recipe specifies BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script.
components.md uses daisyUI-5 classes and contains NONE of the removed-in-v5 classes input-bordered, form-control, or label-text.
preview/showcase.html includes BOTH the daisyUI CDN stylesheet AND the @tailwindcss/browser@4 script, sets data-theme="brand", and uses daisyUI semantic classes.
Includes charts.md with theme-native ApexCharts that read theme colors via var(--color-*) or getComputedStyle, not hardcoded hex.
Ships a SKILL.md (artifact is itself invokable) and a README brand bible; the daisyUI theme is named brand/brand-dark (generic), not the literal brand name.

showcase.html renders as a coherent, energetic themed dashboard with the orange primary on the main CTA/KPIs.
